phenomenon: Exoplanet desert worlds and regolith dynamics
key_measurement: TRAPPIST-1e stellar flux 0.66 S⊕ (Gillon et al., 2017, Nature 542); NASA Exoplanet Archive (June 2026): >5,600 confirmed exoplanets, ~170 rocky (R < 1.6 R⊕) in or near habitable zone
institutions: MIT Kavli Institute for Astrophysics and Space Research (TESS mission)

sources.citation: Gillon, M. et al. (2017) 'Seven temperate terrestrial planets around the nearby ultracool dwarf star TRAPPIST-1', Nature 542:456–460
NASA Exoplanet Archive — Confirmed Exoplanet Catalog, NASA Exoplanet Science Institute, IPAC/Caltech (continuously updated)
Wordsworth, R.D. (2015) 'Atmospheric Heat Redistribution and Collapse on Tidally Locked Rocky Planets', Astrophysical Journal 806(2):180

visualization_compliance: Planet radii, masses, and orbital periods must be drawn from NASA Exoplanet Archive confirmed catalog values — do not use unconfirmed candidates as reference geometry
Atmospheric composition must be labeled HYPOTHETICAL unless confirmed by JWST or HST transmission spectroscopy with cited publication
Tidally locked rotation must be depicted for planets within ~0.05 AU of M-dwarf hosts — do not show day/night rotation for these cases
Regolith grain size and composition must be labeled SPECULATIVE unless supported by reflectance spectroscopy data
Surface temperature ranges must derive from cited energy-balance or GCM models — not interpolated from Earth analogs
Scale bar in AU required for orbital diagrams; planet size scale bar in R⊕ required on comparative size charts
Label ILLUSTRATIVE on all artist surface renderings; do not depict liquid surface water without spectroscopic confirmation
